name: electric_field
description: The electric field strength of the internal electric field of an enzyme along a target bond at the center of the bond.

name: spi
description: The substrate positioning index is defined by the ratio between the solvent-accessible surface area (SASA) of the substrate and the SASA of the enzyme pocket. Studies by the Yang group at Vanderbilt show that it correlates with the enzyme activity that a favorable SPI corresponds to a larger proportion of reactive conformations.

name: active_site_rmsd
description: Active site RMSD is defined as the average of the frame-by-frame root-mean-square deviation of all heavy-atom coordinates of residues that composes the active site of the enzyme relative to an average structure, calculated over the entire molecular-dynamics trajectory.

name: mmpbgbsa
description: The binding energy between the ligand and the protein. Calculate based on the MMPB/GBSA method.

name: ddg_fold
description: The folding free energy of a protein mutant relative to the wild-type.

name: cavity
description: The cavity volume of a binding pocket of the protein.

name: dsi
description: The domain seperation index of the two domains of a bidomain enzyme. This index describes how seperate the two domains are in the dynamic motion of the enzyme. A study by Ning et al showed it is predictive for the cold-adaption ability (the ability to maintain activity at lower temperature) of bidomain enzymes.
